mnemonics.title: Frame Buffer Components
mnemonics.content: FRAME: F-etch R-ead A-ccess M-emory B-uffer E-very D-ata

acronyms.usage: Dedicated memory for graphics frame buffer storage
Flat panel display using liquid crystals
Memory storing depth values for hidden surface removal
Memory specifically designed for graphics cards
Stacked memory technology for high-speed graphics

visualTips.visualization: Think of frame buffer as a 2D grid/matrix where each cell represents a pixel with RGB values stored in memory addresses corresponding to screen positions.
Imagine electron beam drawing horizontal lines from top-left to bottom-right like reading a book page by page, line by line.
Think of Z-buffer as a 'distance map' where smaller values mean closer objects, like layers of transparency stacked from back to front.
Picture two canvas boards - one shown to audience while artist paints on the other, then they swap roles instantly.

analogies.analogy: GPU is like a construction crew with many workers (cores) doing simple tasks in parallel, while CPU is like a single expert manager handling complex tasks sequentially.
Frame buffer is like a digital canvas where every pixel is a paint spot, and the complete painting is stored before being shown.
Refresh rate is like flipbook animation speed - faster flipping (higher Hz) makes motion appear smoother.
Color depth is like the number of crayon colors available - more colors (bits) means more nuanced and realistic drawings.
Resolution is like the number of tiles in a mosaic - more tiles (pixels) means finer details and sharper image.
